channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Uber is replacing Lyft in a points partnership with Delta
item.description: Lyft and Delta are ending their points-earning loyalty partnership in April and the airline will start a new program with rideshare competitor Uber. Uber announced the multi-year strategic partnership on Tuesday evening at the CES tech conference in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta adds Trump ally and UFC boss Dana White to its board
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals
item.description: Outgoing National Cyber Director Harry Coker says ONCD could also lead more efforts governmentwide on cybersecurity planning and response. The post WH national cyber director finalizing software liability proposals first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta's fact-checking changes put company under political microscope
item.description: Meta’s decision to eliminate its fact-checking program marks a sharp reversal for the major social media company, quickly prompting questions over the firm’s direction as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The macOS Dock Turns 25: A Look at its Iconic Design and Industry-Wide Influence
item.description: The macOS Dock celebrates its 25th anniversary, having become an iconic feature in the world of personal computing. Developed by James Thomson, the Dock has influenced industry-wide innovations, shaping Microsoft's taskbar and modern desktop interfaces.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta looking for 'sweetheart deal' with Trump: FTC chair
item.description: Outgoing Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan suggested Tuesday that Meta and other companies facing antitrust lawsuits from the agency might be looking for “sweetheart deals” from the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google warns of legit VPN apps being used to infect devices with malware
item.description: So-called Playfulghost attackers use both SEO poisoning and phishing tactics to spread malware. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: The Financial Reality of ChatGPT Pro - A Loss for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman reveals the company is losing money on its premium chatbot subscription, highlighting the financial challenges of developing advanced AI technology.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI potential meets ROI pragmatism: 3 crucial questions every CIO should ask
item.description: If you had to name 2023’s single-most impactful and disruptive technology, you’d need just two letters: AI. With the release of OpenAI’s ChatGPT in November 2022, we watched a tsunami of AI news and noise throughout the year. And there’s no sign of things slowing down.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Last of Us season 2's new teaser has fans gushing over every detail it contains, but some are also concerned about the welfare of one of its new cast members
item.description: The Last of Us season 2's latest round of footage is full of moments lifted directly from Naughty Dog's video game franchise.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Maingear's newest PC builds are so good I wish I could buy them all, but I'll happily take the Miami Vice one, thanks
item.description: Maingear's latest custom-built gaming PCs are on display at CES 2025, and trust me, you're going to want to see them in person.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: Sony announces new Midnight Black versions of the PS Portal, DualSense Edge, and more PS5 accessories, launching next month
item.description: Sony has unveiled its Midnight Black Collection, a brand new colorway for a range of PS5 accessories.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Think Oura and Samsung's smart rings look good? Ultrahuman just unveiled its Ultrahuman Rare 18K gold and platinum luxury smart rings at CES 2025
item.description: Oura and Samsung rival Ultrahuman has launched smart rings made from real 18K gold and platinum at CES.
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia GeForce Now set to get a lot better on Steam Deck – and that's great news for playing AAA games on Valve's handheld
item.description: If you're a Steam Deck owner craving high-end performance, this could be the best bet while we await a Steam Deck successor.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta scraps fact-checking in favor of X-style Community Notes
item.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged that the changes are in part sparked by political events including Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, The Hill

item.title: El ‘Buscador 360’ que digitaliza la Justicia madrileña supera el 90% de madurez tecnológica
item.description: Hace unos años atrás la Comunidad de Madrid impulsó el desarrollo del proyecto Buscador 360, una solución capaz de transcribir automáticamente las vistas judiciales para que los magistrados, jueces y letrados puedan encontrar de forma rápida y sencilla aquello que se ha dicho durante una sesión.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cybertruck tracking in Las Vegas case raises privacy concerns
item.description: Elon Musk commented on the explosion shortly after it happened, saying it was not an issue with the Cybertruck itself.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, The Hill

item.title: Google faces new labor board complaint over contractor union bargaining
item.description: The US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has filed a fresh complaint against Google, alleging that the company acts as the employer of certain contract workers and must negotiate with their union, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Battle bots: RPA and agentic AI
item.description: I was recently speaking with a client who stated, “We’ve been doing RPA (robotic process automation) for years. What’s the difference with agentic AI? Should I really care, or is it all part of the GenAI hype?” Valid question.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: FBI warns that criminals are using generative AI to exploit individuals with deceptive tactics. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains their tactics and how to protect yourself from them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI redefines customer centricity, beyond language, region for IndiaMART: Nikhil Prabhakar
item.description: In a short span of 28 years, IndiaMART has emerged as India’s chosen B2B marketplace and has increased ease of business while simultaneously creating a connected marketplace for all buyers and suppliers across the country.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Chinese tech companies Tencent, CATL and others protest US listings as army-linked companies
item.description: The U.S. Defense Department has added major Chinese technology companies including gaming and technology company Tencent, artificial intelligence firm SenseTime and the world’s biggest battery maker CATL to its list of companies it says have ties to China’s military.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Your CEO has little idea who IT is or what IT does
item.description: IT may be central to modern existence, but the people and processes of IT remain a mystery to most business executives and colleagues. It’s time to change this. I asked a group of business executives to take out a blank sheet of paper, draw a big circle, and label it “IT People and Processes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sam Altman offers clues about where OpenAI is headed
item.description: On Sunday OpenAI cofounder and CEO Sam Altman published a blog post titled Reflections about his company’s progress—and the speed bumps along the way—during its first nine years.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia’s Project DIGITS puts AI supercomputing chips on the desktop
item.description: Nvidia has built its generative AI (genAI) business on delivering massive computing capacity to data centers where it can be used to train and refine large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Python wins Tiobe language of the year honors
item.description: To the surprise of probably no one, Python has won the Tiobe programming language of the year award for 2024, given to the language with the highest increase in ratings in Tiobe’s index of language popularity during the year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 ransomware predictions, trends, and how to prepare
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz research team has revealed critical insights and predictions on ransomware trends for 2025. The latest Ransomware Report uncovered a surge in sophisticated tactics and extortion attacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
